title: SAP Is Publicly Endorsing Framework Choice for Pro-Code Agent Development
intent: Capture the enterprise shift toward agent ecosystems that allow framework choice rather than a single mandated builder.

source.title: Business AI Innovation Unveiled at SAP TechEd

fact.primary_stat: SAP said on November 17, 2025 that its pro-code approach gives developers freedom to build agents using the framework of their choice, naming LangGraph, CrewAI, and Google’s Agent Development Kit.
supporting_stats: SAP said Joule Agents would become fully compatible with the A2A protocol for collaboration with SAP and non-SAP agents.
SAP also said SAP HANA Cloud MCP support was generally available.
The same announcement positioned centralized control of SAP and non-SAP agents as part of enterprise agent governance.

business_relevance: Even major enterprise vendors are acknowledging that agent development cannot be locked into one builder or one framework.
Developer freedom and governance are being presented together, not as opposing goals.
dzmitryi_commentary: This is close to the advisory space I want to occupy. Businesses need help designing where low-code is enough, where pro-code is required, and how to keep both governable. That is a business and design problem before it becomes a tool problem, especially if the goal is to grow internal expertise instead of outsourcing judgment to a platform.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Capture the enterprise shift toward agent ecosystems that allow framework choice rather than a single mandated builder.
